observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 in recording and costing inventories to present fair balances in the financial statements and conduct physical inventory count to support the balances presented in the financial statements.

We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU, the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to office of the auditor and prepare and maintain individual property ledger cards to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended the following to the Management: a.The Municipal Engineer shall provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; b.The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; c.The OIC-Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card. She shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We recommended the management to prepare and submit to the office of the auditor the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumptions for review as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and implies scope limitation in audit.

We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees and one of the internal control measures that LGUs may adopt to promote good governance at the local level.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Imssplementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.
We commended the management on its efforts to support priority programs and projects of the Department of Education with a recommendation to fully utilize the SEF to maximize the benefits thereof.

We commend the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs and continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

1.6	We recommended the management to (1) require the liquidation of the outstanding cash advance balances by the concerned officials and employees and if no action was taken by them, consider to withhold their salaries, if warranted; (2) no additional cash advances shall be granted to officials and employees with unliquidated balances (3) strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned circular to avoid the penalty stated thereon.

2.5	We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 in recording and costing inventories to present fair balances in the financial statements and conduct physical inventory count to support the balances presented in the financial statements.

3.4	We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.  Also, the Accounting Office should prepare and maintain individual property ledger cards to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The actual inventory report prepared by the GSO/Inventory Committee should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

4.4	We recommended the following to the management:
a. The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
b.	The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
c.	The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

5.5	We recommended that management strictly monitor and fast track the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents. We also recommend the municipal officials adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 of the DILG and DBM in the utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

6.8		We recommended the management to comply with the disaster minimum standards and strictly follow the provisions of RA 10121 to ensure and enhance efficient and effective response before and during disasters.

7.4	We recommended that management fully implement the programs and activities embodied in its LDRRMP in accordance with the provisions of the JMC 2013-1of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG and Section 21 of RA 10121.
8.4	We recommended that management submit the necessary reports for the CY 2021 and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements of the fund.
9.4	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the timely submission of accounts of the agency as prescribed in the above regulation.

10.6	We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the CY 2021 Financial Statements and its supporting schedules to the Office of the Auditor as required by the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

11.4	We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish copies of infrastructure contracts and POs and their supporting documents to the audit team within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Auditor to conduct timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts.

12.6	We recommended that management 1.) ensure the strict implementation of its internal controls; 2.) adopt additional measures or activate the use of the biometric machine to avoid manipulation of the time of arrival in the office; and 3.) require the employees to refund any amount paid in excess of the actual time rendered in service or file for proportionate leave of absence, if warranted.

13.5	We recommended that management stop the charging of cell cards as they already have monthly post-paid plans charged against the funds of the LGU. Also, may we seek justification on the quantity of purchases of the offices using cell cards. Excessive expenses will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

14.5	We recommended that management require the issuance of ICS for all semi-expendable properties of the LGU to properly monitor and establish accountability thereof.  Disbursements with incomplete supporting document(s) will be suspended in audit, if warranted.

15.4	We recommended that management require the payee to explain the use of invalid sales invoice, return the amount reimbursed by her and stop purchasing cell cards as there is a postpaid plan assigned in her office for official communications. Disbursements with invalid supporting document(s) will be disallowed in audit.

16.4	We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

17.2	We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

18.8	We recommended the management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

19.5	We recommended the management to stop the practice of reimbursements in the procurement of goods. For petty expenses, management may adopt the Petty Cash System to avoid reimbursements of small amounts of expenditures. Compliance with the above-cited regulations is enjoined.  Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations may be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

20.4		We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles. Any unnecessary and excessive fuel consumption shall be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

21.3	We recommended that the BAC should ensure that the LGU transact only with bonafide suppliers/dealers of supplies, goods and equipment that the agency want to procure.  We also recommend that management submit justification why the above purchases from non-boafide suppliers will not be disallowed in audit.

22.5	We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees and one of the internal control measures that LGUs may adopt to promote good governance at the local level.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.    Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We recommended that management should always comply with laws and regulations covering the receipt and utilization of funds coming from National Agencies to ensure easier reporting and enhance transparency and accountability in the implementation of the intended programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the LGU strictly adhere to the posting and reporting requirements as provided under DBM Local Budget Circular in the Project Implementation of the BGCM for transparency and accountability on the utilization thereof

We recommended the management to stop procuring goods through cash advances and reimbursements and adhere to the guidelines set under COA and GPPB Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated March 26, 2020 during the State of National Public Health Emergency and COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended the management to return by the General Fund the expenses wrongly charged against BGCM and strictly adhere to the provisions of LBC 125. As to the TEV, please provide justification of the expenses. Any expenses not in line with the accounting and auditing rules and regulations shall be disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.    Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We recommended the management to follow the policies in using Petty Cash Fund, maintain an amount that is sufficient for the recurring petty operating expenses of the LGU for one month and stop the payment of AICS and assistance using PCF.

We recommended the management to comply with the rules and regulations governing the operation of LEEs. Review and assessment shall be done by the LGU regularly to determine whether to continue, modify, expand, scale down, or close the LEE operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that management strictly enforce immediate settlement of audit disallowances to ensure recovery of municipal funds that were either illegally, irregularly or improperly disbursed and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions. Also, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to review her record of disallowances and make an adjustment if necessary.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts, POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA) ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, the MA should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2020 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management to comply with the disaster minimum standards and strictly follow the provisions of RA 10121 to ensure efficient and effective response to disasters.

We recommended the management to observe due care in the use of the LDRRMF to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related PAPs. Also, we recommend that the amount charged against the fund be restituted to avoid the depletion thereof. Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

We recommended the management to provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions listed in Annex F to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended management to stop granting full TA to officials and employees who use government vehicles in the performance of their duties and functions. Pro-rata computation should be observed. Likewise, we recommended that management require the concerned Officials to submit justification, with complete supporting documents, why the corresponding TA paid to them even if they used government vehicle should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended management to stop allowing the issuance of fuel to privately owned vehicles and strictly comply with the provisions of the above-cited regulations. Any unauthorized use or misuse of government funds shall be disallowed in audit, if warranted.
We recommended that management stop the grant of RATA to the PESO, otherwise it will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended the following: a.Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees. It may also as one of the internal control measures that LGU's may adopt to promote good governance at the local level.

We recommended the management to (1) issue final demand letters to the concerned officials and employees and if no action was taken from them, consider to withhold from their salaries, if warranted; (2) no additional cash advances be granted to officials and employees with existing cash advances (3) strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned circulars to avoid the penalty stated thereon.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant complies with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording and costing of inventories of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still failed to use PPSAS guidelines in recording inventories.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 

The HRMO and/or the Accounting Office exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
We recommended that management should strictly comply on the submission of the required reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61.

1.6 We recommended that management should require the Accountable Officers to remit their collections in full, daily to the Liquidating Officer of the municipality. Administrative/disciplinary sanctions should be imposed on the erring AOs should they still fail to strictly comply with the above stated regulation.

2.7 We recommended that the PCF Custodian submit all the supporting documents to liquidate her cash advances for Petty Cash Fund. The Municipal Accountant shall closely monitor the balances of cash advances of the Disbursing Officers and PCF Custodian and see to it that previous cash advances were liquidated before granting new cash advances. Compliance to the above-stated regulation is enjoined.

3.5 We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.    Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

4.4 We recommended that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund of the un-utilized balances. Comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the liquidation of cash advances. We also recommended that management adopt the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by the Disbursing Officer. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees and ensure that the exact amount of payroll was transferred in the account.

5.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
6.5 We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 in recording and costing inventories to present fair balances in the financial statements.

7.5 We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management  conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

8.5 We recommended the following to the management:
The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The General Services Officer shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  She shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

9.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts of the LGU by debiting Other Payables and crediting Due to NGAs for all the fund transfers received from NGAs to reflect reliable account balances in the financial statements.

10.4 We recommended the following:

Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.

The HRMO and/or the Accounting Office exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

11.5 We recommended that the BAC submit copies of approved purchase orders (POs)  irrespective of amount and all related documents forming part thereof to COA within (5) working days upon perfection of contract and issuance of POs.
We recommended that management should strictly comply on the submission of the required reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61.

13.5 We recommended that management stop the payment of phone charges which are personal in nature. If payment has been made, the concerned Head of Offices should pay the municipality in return. Also, the Smart plan should be stopped and changed to Globe Plan to avoid incurrence of additional charges whenever calls are made thereon.  All disbursements in deviation of rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

14.4 We recommended that management implement more strict controls in the payment of wages and salaries by directing the Disbursing Officer thru the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments should only be made directly to the names appearing in the payroll, otherwise, a written authority to collect should be presented by the person claiming the payment.

15.4 We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

16.7 We recommended that management ensure that all disbursements should be properly approved and supported with complete documents.   Strict adherence to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 is enjoined.

17.3 We recommended that management hasten the implementation and identify the reason(s) for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by NGAs.  Management should ensure timely implementation to maximize the benefits derived from the projects.

18.4 We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

19.3 We reiterated our recommendation that the management strictly enforce immediate settlement of audit disallowances to ensure recovery of municipal funds that were either illegally, irregularly or improperly disbursed and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

20.4 We recommended that management require the Accountable Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as basis in the preparation of Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 2 and to ensure that all accountable forms received and issued are properly accounted for. The prompt submission of said monthly report to the Office of the Auditor constitutes timely audit review and verification.

21.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, she should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2019 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same.
22.3 We commended the management for the proper use of the SEF with a recommendation to fully utilize the fund and demand immediate liquidation of cash advances granted to DepEd employees.

23.3 We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted.  Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters for the liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: 5 We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee is in the process of conducting physical count of PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We commended management for the various programs and activities which they implemented with a recommendation for management to comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness specifically on service delivery to ensure that support is in place during emergencies.
observations.managementAction: Expansion of Evacuation Building was done, however, part of the building is still being used by other offices for administrative purposes.

observations.recommendation: We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management strictly enforce immediate settlement of audit disallowances to ensure recovery of municipal funds that were either illegally, irregularly or improperly disbursed and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.
observations.managementAction: Salary deduction has been made on the salaries of some of the employees with disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that the BAC submit copies of approved contracts and purchase orders (POs)  irrespective of amount and all related documents forming part thereof to COA within (5) working days upon perfection of contract and issuance of POs.
observations.managementAction: Management still failed to submit copies of POs/Jos within the prescribed period of time.

observations.recommendation: We commended the management for the proper use of the SEF with a recommendation to demand immediate liquidation of cash advances granted to DepEd employees.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the Manual on Position Classification and Compensation and COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management should ensure that all trip tickets should be properly filled-up and duly approved by the authorized approving officer of the LGU. Any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

We recommend the following:

Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 

The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted.  Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.

We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant complies with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording and costing of inventories of the municipality.

We commended management for the various programs and activities which they implemented with a recommendation for management to comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness specifically on service delivery to ensure that support is in place during emergencies.

We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management strictly enforce immediate settlement of audit disallowances to ensure recovery of municipal funds that were either illegally, irregularly or improperly disbursed and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO and/or the Accounting Office exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended that the BAC submit copies of approved contracts and purchase orders (POs)  irrespective of amount and all related documents forming part thereof to COA within (5) working days upon perfection of contract and issuance of POs.
We commended the management for the proper use of the SEF with a recommendation to demand immediate liquidation of cash advances granted to DepEd employees.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the Manual on Position Classification and Compensation and COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management should ensure that all trip tickets should be properly filled-up and duly approved by the authorized approving officer of the LGU. Any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit, if warranted.
We recommended that management should strictly comply on the submission of the required reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended that all collections be remitted immediately and deposited intact to the authorized depository bank of the agency. Also, the Municipal Treasurer shall closely monitor the remittances and deposits of collections in order to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Administrative sanctions shall be imposed, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Deposits were made the next banking day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and valid reconciling items should be recorded in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.    Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.
observations.managementAction: A bank reconciliation statement was prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued by the Municipal Accountant for immediate liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An inventory committee was created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and General Services Officer endeavor to identify all completed projects funded by other national government agencies for the proper recognition in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The completed projects were recorded on the books through the initiative of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal and Planning Development Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should make a careful perusal of the aforementioned accounts payables with the view of reverting the same to the Government Equity Account if warranted.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the proper recognition and recording of inventories to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management evaluate the status of project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by NGAs. Unutilized balance of the funds should be returned in accordance with the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement with the source agencies.

We recommended that management monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Biddings for various projects were initiated by the Local Chief Executive through the Bids and Awards Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the LGU be allotted and used for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The 20% development fund was disbursed only for priority development PPAs.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements on the utilization of the LDRRM Fund.

We commended the management in implementing various PPAs which are supportive of GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that management should ensure that vehicles of the municipal government are properly marked with “For Official Use Only” as prescribed under COA Circular No. 75-6 and all trips made thereon are properly monitored with corresponding documentations.
observations.managementAction: Government issued vehicles were already marked with for official use only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman to submit copies of approved contracts and purchase orders (POs)  irrespective of amount and all related documents forming part thereof to COA within (5) working days upon perfection of contract and issuance of POs.
We commended the management for its efforts in implementing programs and projects that protect children’s rights in the municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of the Municipal Development Council fast track the implementation of the projects/programs and activities under the 20% Development Fund as programmed to achieve the desired benefits to be derived from such projects. observations.managementAction: Many projects remain unimplemented as at year-end and were implemented only on the first quarter of 2018.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly monitor and require the collectors to regularly remit intact all their collections to avoid misapplication of government funds and to reflect all income collected during the year. Strictly require the   Cashier to returned/deposit all unutilized cash advances for petty/payroll/operating expenses to the authorized depository bank every end of the year.

observations.reason: Reiterated as finding number 1.

” An order creating the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all fixed assets owned by the Local Government Unit and defining their functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the  LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.

observations.reason: Reiterated as finding number 11.

observations.managementAction: Management decided to cancel one contract for the failure of the contractor to implement the project on time.
observations.reason: Some projects remain unimplemented as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management particularly the Municipal Treasurer  transfer/deposit in full the LDRRM Fund from the surplus of the general fund otherwise; concerned officials are liable for technical malversation.
observations.managementAction: The correct amount of LDRRMF was transferred to its proper account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly monitor the attendance and whereabouts of its personnel. Undertimes/tardiness incurred by the employees must be deducted from the payroll.
observations.managementAction: A Memorandum Order was issued ordering the HRMO to strictly monitor the implementation of CSC Rules and Regulations particularly on the official working hours.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management publish tax declaration numbers of delinquent taxpayers so that they will be informed of their obligations. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to review the list of delinquent taxpayers because from the confirmation made, some has already settled their accounts. The ultimate goal of improving the collection efficiency and effectiveness ratio of RPT is not remote with the collective efforts of the legislative council by supporting the local revenue code, the office of the Municipal Assessor by undertaking timely revisions and simultaneous cleansing of the RPTAS database and the office of the Municipal Treasurer by strengthening the force of law in availing itself of other more effective and powerful remedies so provided in enforcing collection of delinquent accounts such as administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action.
observations.managementAction: The stall rentals amounting to    P 82,146.75 were already collected as of May 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees and ensure that the exact amount of payroll was transferred in the account.
observations.managementAction: Management already coordinated with the bank regarding the use of ATM in their payroll system.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management 

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all funds to be able to identify reconciling items and possible erroneous recordings and postings of transactions in the books and the banks, and strictly adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly bank reconciliation monthly to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: Bank statements were submitted but subsidiary ledgers were not prepared.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding number 2.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that officials and employees of the municipal government with outstanding cash advance be directed to immediately liquidate/settle their cash advances to avoid the possibility of being charged due to misuse of government funds. If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations. Henceforth, monitor closely all outstanding cash advances and demand immediately the liquidations. Management should strictly enforce the time limits in the liquidation of cash advances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant  to give extra effort in determining the creditors of the unaccounted cash advances of P 1.5 million otherwise, they will be held personally liable thereof. Avail of the remedies provided under COA Circular No. 2016-004 relative to writing off of dormant cash advances giving priority to those pertaining to deceased officials and employees with still unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters for immediate liquidation of unliquidated cash advances
were issued by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding number 3.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management evaluate the amount of bond coverage and whenever necessary, increase their coverage with the Fidelity Bond of the BT especially the Municipal Treasurer/Assistant Municipal Treasurer  since they are handling large sums of money turned over to them by the collecting officers pending deposit to the authorized depository bank. Require all accountable officers handling cash to have cash book for recording their receipts and deposits of collections.
observations.managementAction: Fidelity bond of the  accountable officers were already increased and duly approved by the Bureau of Treasury dated March 27, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management restore the funds of the LDDRMF that were irregularly disbursed for purposes other than it was provided for, and to ensure that the LDRRMF  are exclusively disbursed for projects and activities that are related to disaster, relief and recovery programs and activities so as not to compromise the implementation of the projects or activities under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and Appropriation.
observations.managementAction: JMC No. 2013-1 were strictly followed as to the disbursements of 5% calamity fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management discontinue the practice of appropriating part of the 5% Gender and Development Fund and other Local Funds for the members of sangguniang bayan’s discretionary fund. Strictly utilize the GAD in accordance with Joint Circular 2004 - 01.
observations.managementAction: GAD Fund was utilized in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management  adhere to the provision of RA 7160 and the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 the Revised Guidelines on the use of the Special Education Fund (SEF). We also recommended that management avoid procurement through cash advances.
We recommended that the management enforce the full settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances from the persons deemed liable.
observations.managementAction: Settlements for various NDs were made for calendar year 2017.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding number 15.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in the classification of receipts made by the LGU. Draw the necessary journal entry voucher to reclassify other payable accounts to income and government equity accounts that were understated by P317, 753.41.

observations.managementAction: Posting in the PHILGEPS for government procurements was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of procuring regular office supplies by department/office, instead require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all needed supplies of departments/offices of the municipality based on their individual Project Procurement Plan (PPMP) to the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). Based on the APP, the BAC should adopt bulk purchases through PS DBM and if ever there is no available item resort through private suppliers through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the municipality pursuant to RA 9184 and ITS Revised IRR in order to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement of the office supplies.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of regular office supplies were consolidated for procurement.

The Municipal Mayor has already instructed the official and employees to prepare trip tickets and travel order in order to determine whether the travel is official before issuing a requisition slip for fuel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Formulate implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid or assistance to various individuals in crisis situation for uniformity and fairness as well as to avoid possible misuse of government funds. 

Issue Executive Order   to harmonize the granting of assistance and at the same time, avoid the inequitable allocation of financial aid to the personnel and its constituents of the municipality.  

c) Appropriate financial assistance program under   PPA only for proper monitoring and reporting.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan enacted Resolution No. 28, 2017 series of 2017 A Resolution creating a Policy Governing the Processes involved in the Release and Setting the Parameters for Grants and Donations

We reiterated our recommendation that the management prepare collection notice/billing for   the outstanding loan receivable so that the loan repayment shall be collected from the Cabarroguis Water District.

We also recommended that the cost of the dilapidated old building be dropped from the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the  LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that the management restore the funds of the LDDRMF that were irregularly disbursed for purposes other than it was provided for, and to ensure that the LDRRMF  are exclusively disbursed for projects and activities that are related to disaster, relief and recovery programs and activities so as not to compromise the implementation of the projects or activities under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and Appropriation.

We recommended that management discontinue the practice of appropriating part of the 5% Gender and Development Fund and other Local Funds for the members of sangguniang bayan’s discretionary fund. Strictly utilize the GAD in accordance with Joint Circular 2004 - 01.

We recommended that management  adhere to the provision of RA 7160 and the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 the Revised Guidelines on the use of the Special Education Fund (SEF). We also recommended that management avoid procurement through cash advances.
We recommended that the management enforce the full settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances from the persons deemed liable.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in the classification of receipts made by the LGU. Draw the necessary journal entry voucher to reclassify other payable accounts to income and government equity accounts that were understated by P317,753.41.

We recommended that the management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009 001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended that the management limit the payment of BAC honoraria to the extent from sale of bid documents and stop using other funds for payments thereof. Strictly adhere to the provisions of   Budget Circular No. 2007-3.

) The Bids and Awards Committee  ensure the posting of the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contracts in the PHILGEPS and in the website of the LGU for all their procurement activities.
) The BAC prepare Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurement at the end of each semester and have the report approved by the HOPE.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of procuring regular office supplies by department/office, instead require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all needed supplies of departments/offices of the municipality based on their individual Project Procurement Plan (PPMP) to the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). Based on the APP, the BAC should adopt bulk purchases through PS DBM and if ever there is no available item resort through private suppliers through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the municipality pursuant to RA 9184 and ITS Revised IRR in order to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement of the office supplies.
We also recommended that management restore the funds of the LDRRMF that were irregularly disbursed for Solid Waste Management Program.

The  Barangay Captain is encouraged to make proper representation with the Provincial and Municipal Government, as conditioned by necessity, for the improvement of the BHU facilities and to address concerns of patients, health workers and its constituents. Also, increase the supply of medicines to the barangay for the constituents and address the issuer regarding the potable water supply and water sealed toilets.

We recommended that management:
a)  Formulate implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid or assistance to various individuals in crisis situation for uniformity and fairness as well as to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
b) Issue Executive Order   to harmonize the granting of assistance and at the same time, avoid the inequitable allocation of financial aid to the personnel and its constituents of the municipality.
c) Appropriate financial assistance program under   PPA only for proper monitoring and reporting.

We reiterated our recommendation that management 1) evaluate and review the existing fund balances in order to determine the proper disposition of these dormant trust collections/cash advances; 2) coordinate with the source agencies for proper disposition thereof, otherwise, request for realignment of these funds to  utilize the same for beneficial or meaningful projects.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management prepare collection notice/billing for   the outstanding loan receivable so that the loan repayment shall be collected from the Cabarroguis Water District.

We recommended that  
the Municipal Mayor, 
being the Chairman of 
the Municipal 
Development Council 
implement the 
projects/programs and 
activities under the 
20% Development Fund 
as programmed to 
achieve the desired 
benefits to be derived
from such projects.

The 
unimplemented 
projects were 
programmed for 
2016
observations.reason:
Some projects 
were still 
on-going.
Reiterated as 
finding no. 2.

of the municipal
government with

Henceforth, monitor

demand immediately the
liquidations. Management

advances.
We also recommended
that the Municipal
Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant
and Municipal
Treasurer to determine
the creditors of the
unaccounted cash
advances of P2.0
million otherwise,
they will be held
personally liable
thereof.
